summary: Completed testing of 1:1 Document-to-Requirement Mapping system. All 13 backend tests pass. Frontend UI verified: Documents tab shows one row per requirement, Upload/Replace buttons work correctly, version numbers increment on re-upload, CV and Reference 1/2 appear as separate requirements. Fixed bug where verified_by was showing user_id instead of user name.
passed_tests: Backend: compliance-requirements endpoint returns valid data
Backend: One row per requirement (no duplicates)
Backend: Reference 1 and Reference 2 appear as separate requirements
Backend: Document requirements have correct structure
Backend: Verification status shows correctly with verified_by name
Backend: Summary statistics are calculated correctly
Backend: Document status values are valid
Backend: All mandatory document types present
Backend: Compliance endpoint for checklist works
Backend: Checklist items have correct structure
Frontend: Documents tab shows one row per requirement
Frontend: Upload button appears for missing requirements
Frontend: Replace button appears for existing documents
Frontend: Verification status shows 'Verified by System Admin'
Frontend: Checklist tab shows requirement status correctly
updated_files: /app/backend/server.py (fixed verified_by_name to use 'name' field instead of 'full_name', and store verified_by_name in database)
seed_data_creation: Used existing employee Olakunle Alonge (id: d88335f6-1b18-435a-8086-28af4a583f77). Test documents uploaded for DBS and Reference 2 requirements.
context_for_next_testing_agent: 1:1 Document-to-Requirement Mapping fully functional. Documents tab shows one row per requirement with Upload/Replace buttons. Version numbers increment on re-upload. CV and Reference 1/2 appear as separate requirements. Fixed bug where verified_by was showing user_id - now shows user name correctly.

bug_fixed.issue: verified_by was showing user_id (e.g., 'user_d91e02377f78') instead of user name
bug_fixed.root_cause: verify_employee_document endpoint was looking for 'full_name' field but user documents have 'name' field. Also, verified_by_name was not being stored in the database.
fix_applied: Changed 'full_name' to 'name' in user lookup query
Added verified_by_name to update_data so it's stored in database
Now shows 'System Admin' instead of user_id
